title: How to prepare Botox for injection
filename: How to prepare Botox for injection.en.vtt
excerpt: So I want to put saline into this file and get the saline in without causing any additional bubbles because that just makes it annoying when you're trying to get your botronym toxin out so a nice smooth transfer of saline into this file is the goal and the problem is there's a va…

segments.text: so I want to put saline into this file and get the saline in without causing any additional bubbles because that just makes it annoying when you're trying to get your botronym toxin out so a nice smooth transfer of saline into this file is the goal and the problem is there's a vacuum in here so this will suck saline out really quite quickly so I need to think about how I can control that otherwise what you end up with is a frothy mess it's like a badly poured pint if you don't do this correctly and it's okay because as soon as you release the vacuum most of those bubbles will go anyway but I just like you know it's one of those things when you do it ten thousand times you learn how to do it in a way that is just more controlled and feels nicer and that's what the goal is today so top little plastic bottle on top that's useful because you know when you've opened it or not this will now never go back on in quite the same way so you know it's a freshly opened vial then we'll um I like to use a green needle there's lots of ways of doing this but if you

release the pressure first by putting the needle in you'll hear a little that tells me that's now at neutral and I can now take my 2.5 mils of saline out of this and I can slowly drip it into here and I can pop like you would with a pint you can put it on the edge so that it trickles in nice and slowly now because this I've now made it neutral and now I'm now putting 2.5 mils of volume in it will actually build up pressure if I had another needle in the top I would you'd hear another as it entered as it left but I don't so that's okay but I'm now I'm you must take your needle back out of that solution otherwise it'll squirt back up into your syringe so I need this to be out and then see how that comes up on its own but I would just normally hold that down and come out and that's it now I just want to make sure it's mixed well so a little swish round is all you need and I don't want to get bubbles in it I'm just swirling it around

and that should be good to go and I like to take the rubber band off the top as well so this will now be easily accessible I'll pop my BD syringe in I'll show you how to do that in a second the first thing I want to do is find a little Gap in the label where I can see all the way down so that tells me where the meniscus is because I need to make sure that my needle is under that line if you haven't done that then you draw bubbles in so I then will slide it down here and I can see it not touching the glass that's the next mistake people make as you touch the glass if you touch the glass with a needle you instantly blend from that needle and becomes very unpleasant to be injected with you got this crunching sound which many people think is normal because I've only ever had it um but actually there's no need for that so now I'm underneath the meniscus I've got zero air in my syringe and I will pull up using my thumb and this I'm making this look easy because I've done it 10 000 times but it's not like a lot of people will really struggle at this stage and it looks very tricky so I'm just drawing up like that if you've never done this before you

will move way too much and you will lose your position so I'll do a little check for any of the Little bubbles so I have actually got a little bubble here and that is unacceptable if I inject with that will cause the air the pressure problem so I need to do little gentle flicks and they are gentle just to try and dislodge it so once this is lodged I can pull back and I've now got another one higher up great I'm actually using the little floppy bit of my glove to do most of the flicking it's not actually a very hard flick when you pull the syringe back the meniscus often allows the bubbles to pop and that's a good way to get the last few ones out as a bubble free syringe
